Explanation: The 2016 paper "Death of embryos from 2300-year-old quinoa seeds found in an archaeological site" by H.P. Burrieza et al. states that quinoa seeds excavated in the 1970s at Los Morrillos, San Juan, Argentina, were radiocarbon dated to around 2300 years old [42206]. Biographical clues (degrees between 1971-1982, association with South American universities, research on autophagy, extremophiles, and a daughter named Julieta) match Hugo Pablo Burrieza, who is affiliated with Universidad de Buenos Aires and has a daughter named Julieta. This aligns with Agent 2 and Agent 3's findings.
Exact Answer: 2300 years; Hugo Pablo Burrieza
Confidence: 100%
